title: Launch admission and merge reconciliation
goal: Make run admission and serial integration merge recoverable daemon-owned protocols with durable intent, compensation, and restart reconciliation.
acceptance: Use behavioral TDD: first add fault/concurrency tests that leave partial launch activation or an ambiguous post-cherry-pick state, demonstrate the failures, then implement recoverable protocols.
Run admission is a transactional StateStore command with a durable uniqueness guarantee for one active run per canonical repository, independent of the daemon process mutex.

Admission records a launch intent before branch/worktree/cockpit activation. Every subsequent failure either compensates created resources and resolves the intent or leaves a durable reconcilable state with the primary cause.
Daemon restart reconciles incomplete launch intents idempotently and never creates duplicate active runs, integration branches, worktrees, or worker launches.
Each serial integration operation persists a merge intent containing run, slice, attempt, source commit, expected integration head, and operation identity before Git mutation.

After cherry-pick/merge and before marking completion, the daemon durably records the resulting head and state/event transition. Restart reconciliation uses commit ancestry/tree and the journal to distinguish not-started, applied, conflicted, and divergent states without reapplying an already integrated commit.
Merge conflicts, abort failures, branch deletion failures, and state-write failures retain structured evidence and a deterministic operator/recovery path; they do not silently advance checkpoints.
Tests inject failures at every admission and merge boundary, include two concurrent daemon clients/processes, and prove restart convergence, no duplicate merges, and no leaked active-run lock.
must_ask_if: The design would treat Git reflog or renderer text as the sole authoritative merge journal.
Recovery would auto-discard operator commits or force-reset a divergent integration branch.
Admission uniqueness requires a new external coordination service instead of SQLite/filesystem/Git state already owned by the daemon.
The fix would change dependency ordering, slice authority, or serial integration policy.
